title: Rainy Glass
description: Adds realistic and pixel version of rain to glass panes.
body:
![Rainy Glass Mod Version 1.2.2 with rainy pane background](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/81716cd5e238b74d796dd378144d4dc6785c596d.jpeg)
## **Requirements (Important)**
### No shaders requirements:
None, install fabric, the mod, and play.﻿

### Shader Requirements:
Requires Bliss 2.0.4 or Unstable (can be found on Bliss GitHub Unstable Branch) for the rain shading to work, other shaders may not always work. Enable rain refractions and reflections.

### Recommended:
(Requires the Bliss shader block.properties file to be modified with block ID's if you want clouds to render properly)
Located in .minecraft > shaderpacks > Bliss.zip > Bliss > Shaders > block.properties > ctrl+F to search:

####### ----- reflective translucents / glass ----- #######

block.301=glass glass_pane glass_rain_mod:glass_normal glass_rain_mod:glass_rainy \


## **Description**
This mods adds rain to glass panes that will look realistic or in Minecraft style. The rain droplets are only specific to the new rain-pane block. The rain-panes can be created by placing a Mysterious Geode block next to regular Minecraft glass panes. Mysterious Geodes can be found by harvesting Suspicious Stone ore in the caves.


## **Features**
- Realistic and Pixel rain
- New Ore
- Two new decorative blocks
- New glass pane style


## **FAQ**
### Why make this?
I wanted Minecraft to feel more aesthetic when it rains and to hopefully create a push to make rain shaders a default feature in shader packs if possible.

### My rain is all pixelated.
Disable the Built-In resource pack, the pixelated rain is integrated and on by default since 1.2.2.

(Ask more questions in issue tracker page comments)
